description: Goal: reduce the quoted-source/macro/source-surface spine to one coherent model: quoted roots are the only source authority, macro expansion is root->root over one heap, publication is one transaction, and compiler2 type/spec/extern fragments travel as preserved token payloads instead of being reconstructed or re-lexed. Signal: ordinary source forms, item-macro expansions, and Fz.Compiler.define all feed the same publication engine; expansion is separated from publication; compiler2 no longer re-renders quoted type fragments back into tokens; the relevant fz2 source-surface fixtures become tractable without bespoke paths. Strategy: first collapse duplicate publication logic, then split expansion from publication, then standardize fragment transport, then narrow quote/unquote lowering, and finally remove retired code paths and restate the design in docs/telemetry.
title: Compiler2 source spine cleanup: unify publication, expansion, and fragment transport
